textPlain: > 23aiOracle's tradition of tacking on $CURRENT_TREND suffixes to its DB versions is, has always been, and always will be cringy.
This post is mostly an ad for their migration services?.

It'd be interesting to see what percentage of companies signed up with Larry stick with Larry out of sheer momentum. Sure, proprietary DBMSes can be faster and more efficient, but I can't imagine they'd be so much more efficient as to justify the license fee, so something else would have to be the justification.

It's for the support in most cases.But last time I really used Oracle's RDBMS (10g era) it still had capabilities that no open source database had. If you really needed that, there wasn't an easy substitute. I'm sure Postgres has narrowed the gap by now.

Any one of: the risk of a migration going wrong is too high, application or infrastructure compatibility issues, or the cost of retraining staff who work with the database to work with something else.It is possible that there are simple solutions to these problems, but the perception that they are serious will turn companies away from a migration.
